mnemonics: DANCING DENDRITES: Dendrites Detect Data, Axons Announce Answers. Neurons Never nap; they're always firing and wiring.
keyPoints: A biological neuron consists of a cell body (soma), dendrites (input receivers), an axon (output transmitter), and synapses (connection points).
Neurons communicate via electrical impulses (action potentials) and chemical signals (neurotransmitters) across synapses.
The strength of connections between neurons (synaptic plasticity) is the basis for learning and memory in the brain.
commonMistakes: Thinking a single neuron fires for a complex concept; it's the pattern of firing across a vast network that encodes information.
Equating the all-or-nothing nature of an action potential with a simple binary (0/1) signal, ignoring the critical role of firing rate and timing.
